title: Ton Phanan
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=New Republic |image=[[File:Ton_Phanan.jpg]] |name=Ton Phanan |homeworld=[[Rudrig/Legends|Rudrig]]''[[X-Wing: Wraith Squadron]]'' |birth=Before [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]]{{Blog|holocron|13/comments|Major Character Birth Years}} places [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles's]] [[Reproduction/Legends|birth]] in [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]]. Ton Phanan was born before Antilles, according to ''[[X-Wing: Wraith Squadron]]''. |death=[[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' [[Halmad/Legends|Halmad]]''[[X-Wing: Iron Fist]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]]{{InsiderCite|83|20 Most Memorable Moments of the Expanded Universe}} |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Fair |cyber=*[[Cybernetics/Legends|Cybernetics]] on left side of face with built-in [[Comm unit/Legends|comm unit]] *Cybernetic left [[Eye/Legends|eye]] *Cybernetic left [[Limb/Legends|leg]] *Cybernetic right hand |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] **[[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] **[[Wraith Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Starting over means more time. More time for Zsinj to bombard more colonies, to destroy more ships. Another day may mean some bright young doctor gets it the way I did and ends up what I am.''"
"''What you are is pretty good.''"
"''Not as good as some kid with a superior intellect whose only aim is to make people better. I'd rather he be out there than me.|Ton Phanan and Garik Loran|}} '''Ton Phanan''' was a [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]] from [[Rudrig/Legends|Rudrig]] who joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Phanan was badly wounded while serving aboard a [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]] at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]; allergic to [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]], he had to accept [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] replacement parts to survive. Phanan fell into a deep depression after this incident but used his strong sense of humor to present a cheery front to those around him. Phanan transferred to [[New Republic Starfighter Command/Legends|New Republic Starfighter Command]], where he quickly joined [[Wraith Squadron]]. Among the Wraiths, Phanan was assigned [[Garik Loran]] as a [[Wingmate/Legends|wingman]]. Kindred spirits, they quickly became mutual best friends, spreading their sense of humor throughout the unit. Phanan served with distinction in the commando [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]], aiding in the destruction of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Apwar Trigit]]'s forces, helping root out a [[Black market/Legends|black market]] ring aboard a training frigate, fighting as one of the [[Hawk-bat Independent Space Force|Hawk-bat]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] in a [[Hunt for Zsinj|deception]] designed to bring down [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]], and constantly raising morale. In a mission over [[Halmad/Legends|Halmad]], however, Phanan was shot down and grievously injured. When the other [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] had to evacuate, Loran stayed behind to rescue his friend. He recovered Phanan, but the doctor passed away as Loran tried to spirit him to safety. In his last hours, however, Phanan composed a message to Loran telling his best friend to exorcise his own personal demons as Phanan had never managed to, a lesson Loran took to heart. ==Biography== ===Doctor Phanan=== {{Quote|I used to be Dr. Phanan. Fully licensed to cut you open and weld you shut again.''"
"''Why did you give it up?''"
"''Because I didn't care for patching up people I don't care about and do enjoy killing people I hate.|Ton Phanan and Tyria Sarkin|}} Ton Phanon was born on [[Rudrig/Legends|Rudrig]] in the [[Tion Hegemony/Legends|Tion Hegemony]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} at some point prior to [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]] and was the only child of wealthy parents who raised him late in life. Phanan grew up developing a strong sense of humor and, as a schoolboy, an interest in insects; he liked them because they made girls squeamish. Eventually, Phanan became a licensed [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]]; before he graduated, both his parents had died, leaving him with no living relatives of any closeness and a large inheritance. He practiced for years and joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the early years of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. As a Rebel, Phanan served as a doctor aboard an [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] [[Frigate/Legends|frigate]]. Phanan was present at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], at which his frigate was fired upon and he was wounded badly, losing a [[Limb/Legends|leg]] and suffering major trauma to his head and other injuries. Phanan was allergic to [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]], and so his wounds had to be replaced with [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetics]]; by [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]] he was twenty percent cybernetic. Phanan began to feel that his future had been lost and eventually decided that the healing profession was not for him; he received no enjoyment from it, and instead turned to a trade in which he could strike back against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], rather than heal those he had no like for. He applied for a transfer to, and was accepted into, the [[New Republic Starfighter Corps/Legends|New Republic Starfighter Corps]]. ===Becoming a Wraith=== {{Quote|I have a bit of a problem with luck in combat. Unlike most of you, I've seen some of it—''"
"''Braggart.''"
"''But in five live-fire missions, I've been shot down twice and landed successfully three times. Not a good ratio. Between that and all the new prosthetics, I'm sort of an expensive proposition for any commander.|Ton Phanan and Tyria Sarkin|}} [[File:GarikLoran-NEGTC.png|thumb|left|180px|Garik Loran, Ton Phanan's best friend]] Joining the Starfighter Corps, Phanan was able to fly five combat missions; in two of them, he was shot down. Combined with his inability to be treated with bacta, he was considered a liability for his unit and his career prospects were dim. In 7 ABY, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] decided that the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] needed a new type of fighter unit, a commando team cross-trained to fly fighters as well; he felt that it would create a more versatile unit that could accomplish more missions on its own. He was able to get [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] to sign off on the plan, and to avoid having to train new [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]], he requested washouts, troublemakers, and pilots otherwise on the verge of being kicked out the service, giving them one last chance to prove themselves. Hearing of this, Phanan requested a transfer to this new unit and reported to [[Folor Base]], where Antilles reviewed the candidates. Phanan quickly made friends with [[Garik Loran]], who shared his extravagant sense of humor. They flew together in exercises, and after one simulation they proceeded to the [[DownTime]] [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]], where they met fellow candidates [[Kell Tainer]], [[Tyria Sarkin Tainer|Tyria Sarkin]], [[Voort saBinring|Voort "Piggy" saBinring]], and [[Hohass Ekwesh|Hohass "Runt" Ekwesh]]. Tainer was able to figure out that Loran was actually Garik "Face" Loran, the former Imperial [[Propaganda/Legends|propaganda]] [[Holodrama/Legends|holodrama]] child star, a fact that greatly amused Phanan. Phanan jokingly flirted with Sarkin, who was eventually offended and left when Loran made light of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], for which she had a minor aptitude. The pair made friends with the other pilot-candidates, however, and all of them, Sarkin included, made the final unit roster; Phanan had the fourth-highest scores of the group, behind Tainer, Loran, and [[Falynn Sandskimmer]]. Phanan would serve as the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]]—temporarily dubbed Gray Squadron—medic and was given the callsign Gray Seven, with Loran as his [[Wingmate/Legends|wingman]] and Tainer leading the flight. Ekwesh filled out Two Flight as Tainer's wingman. The next morning, Two Flight set out in the unit's four new [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]] for a live-fire exercise, launching [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedos]] at a target strung behind the [[Narra (shuttle)|''Narra'']], the squadron's support [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]]. They operated in wing pairs; when Phanan and Loran opened their strike foils, Phanan lost control of his weapons systems and Loran's [[Targeting computer/Legends|targeting]] [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] failed. Phanan immediately improvised, feeding Loran his own targeting data; the pair scored a kill on the target. They continued with the training, Phanan receiving his [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]], [[Gadget]], until Antilles deemed the unit, now named [[Wraith Squadron]], ready for a navigational exercise to [[Doldrums]]. As the squadron was about to leave [[Folor]], however, squadron comm specialist [[Jesmin Ackbar]] detected Imperial comm activity. Determining that [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]]'s subordinate Admiral [[Apwar Trigit]] was about to attack the base, the Wraiths returned and prepared to defend the facility. Along with the [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptor]] [[Blue Squadron (New Republic)|Blue Squadron]], the Wraiths set up position in the [[Trench (Folor)|Pig Trough]] canyon, ready to ambush the Imperial forces as they approached the base. They were successful, eliminating many enemy [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] as the base was safely evacuated; once the last [[Transport/Legends|transport]] was away, they too evacuated—to Doldrums. Phanan was struck by shrapnel and sustained a minor injury in the battle, but was able to treat it himself. On their way to Doldrums, they were pulled out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] early and struck by a particularly powerful [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion]] blast in the [[Xobome system/Legends|Xobome system]]. Phanan's fighter was rendered completely inoperable; the ion charge also wreaked havoc on his cybernetics. He was evacuated to the ''Narra'', and his fighter was gutted to build the ''[[Lunatic]]'', a jury-rigged armed compartment. This crude and hastily contrived device was designed to allow fellow squadron mate Voort saBinring to infiltrate the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] they assumed would follow up on the triggering of the [[Mine/Legends|mine]] which had disabled them. The gambit was successful, and saBinring was able to force the crew of the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] [[Ession Strike|''Night Caller'']] to surrender when he took control of its bridge. Phanan, along with the rest of the squadron, transferred aboard the ship to patch himself up fully and work on bringing his prosthetics back to full functionality. ===Aboard the ''Night Caller''=== {{Quote|I only met him a few weeks ago. But by the end of the second day, we were finishing one another's sentences and being obnoxious enough to drive everyone around us crazy.|Garik Loran, to Lara Notsil, speaking of Ton Phanan|}} As the crew of the ''Night Caller'' had not had time to send out a distress message, Trigit had no idea the ship under his command had been captured. Antilles stationed Wraith Squadron aboard the ship and brought in a New Republic crew to have it make its appointed rounds, maintaining the [[Hunt for Zsinj|subterfuge]] that the ship was still serving Zsinj. While serving aboard the ship, Phanan and Tainer approached Sarkin in an attempt to get her to loosen up from her intense training. When unit [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]] [[Eurrsk Thri'ag]] approached and jokingly offered to boost her scores by slicing them, Sarkin suddenly snapped and attacked him. Phanan and Tainer, shocked, went to visit her after the incident and she confessed to them that she had been on the verge of flunking out of the training academy when her trainer, [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Atton Repness]], approached her with the offer to raise her grades if she cooperated with a plan to eject from an X-wing following supposed equipment failure; he would in fact retain the fighter to sell on the [[Black market/Legends|black market]] for profit. She said she would turn him in, but he revealed that her grades had long been below failing and that turning him in would cause her to wash out; faced with this, she had reluctantly agreed to keep quiet but felt lingering shame over having given in. The two agreed to keep this secret. The ship continued making its rounds, with Wraith Squadron striking Zsinj's covert assets after ''Night Caller'' visited and discovered them; Antilles hoped to lure Zsinj into setting a trap for the Wraiths, who were apparently following the ''Night Caller'', and turn the tables on the warlord. On [[Xartun]], the Wraiths targeted a [[Transparisteel/Legends|transparisteel]] factory supplying Zsinj, sneaking in at night to sabotage it. Phanan was standing guard with Sarkin and Ekwesh when they were ambushed by a [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droid]]; Ekwesh was knocked unconscious while Phanan and Sarkin fired on the droid, but it was too heavily armored for them to damage it. Phanan fired on it to cover Sarkin as she backed a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] cart into it to pin it; Ekwesh awoke and was able to put two more carts into place, trapping it long enough for them to escape before Tainer set off his explosives to destroy the factory. At [[M2398 system|M2398]], the ''Night Caller'' was [[Battle of M2398-3|ambushed]] by a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] gang who had decided not to take Zsinj up on his offer of alliance. Phanan launched along with the rest of the pilots; his X-wing had not yet been replaced, but he used Sandskimmer's, as she was piloting one of ''Night Caller''{{'s}} [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]]. In the battle, Jesmin Ackbar was killed; Tainer had attempted to save her by using his own fighter to push her disabled one off course, but her trailing power cables disabled his fighter and her ship crashed. Tainer suffered an anxiety attack and reported to Phanan in sick bay; Phanan had already treated [[Myn Donos]] for what he claimed to be a concussion. In fact, Donos had frozen up after the loss of his astromech, [[Shiner]], the only survivor of Donos's previous command, [[Talon Squadron (New Republic)|Talon Squadron]], and had not exited his barely lucid state. Realizing that reporting this would destroy Donos's career, Phanan instead kept it from Antilles and second-in-command [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]] for several days until Loran, Tainer, Sarkin, and Sandskimmer were able to arrange an intervention in which they helped Donos overcome his feelings of guilt over the loss of his former squadron. When Sarkin began dating Tainer, Phanan was somewhat annoyed, having found her highly attractive. ===Trapping Trigit=== {{Quote|Yes, I got so angry that I had to vent my spleen.|Ton Phanan, to Kell Tainer, after having his spleen punctured|}} When Zsinj assigned the ''Night Caller'' to rendezvous with another of his ships, the [[Hawkbat (starship)|''Hawkbat'']], to take on supplies before participating in an attack on New Republic forces at [[Talasea]], the Wraiths had to plan quickly to avoid having their deception exposed. Phanan worked with Antilles to develop a plan in which they would infect the ''Hawkbat''{{'s}} crew with some sort of contagious disease, leading the crew to avoid face-to-face contact. They decided to acquire the infectious agent on [[Storinal]], where the ''Hawkbat''{{'s}} crew would have leave. They landed at [[Revos]], all in disguise, and Phanan proceeded to the capital city, [[Scohar]], with Sarkin and Tainer in order to procure the disease from the [[Scohar Xenohealth Institute]], where many samples of diseases were kept for study. Phanan searched for possible agents held there, and decided to use [[Bunkurd Sewer Disorder]], a stomach ailment. With Thri'ag added to their group, the four infiltrated the Xenohealth Institute through its waste-disposal chute and retrieved two samples of BSD. While in the Xenohealth Institute, they had to pass through an area containing various small creatures; Phanan stole a [[Storini glass prowler]] for a pet. They then planted the BSD in the air intakes of the two shuttles which would carry the ''Hawkbat'' crew back up to [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], before stealing two TIEs from the local [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] in order to fill out the ''Night Caller''{{'s}} TIE complement and escaping. [[File:CroppedWraiths.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Ton Phanan at the center of several other Wraiths]] When they set up a pair of [[Spy/Legends|spy]] [[Satellite/Legends|satellites]] as part of their ''Night Caller'' subterfuge, hoping to record the unit supposedly following them, Phanan and Loran flew cover for the [[Vacuum/Legends|vacuum]]-suited workers. Before taking off, Loran discovered a small insect in his cockpit; thinking it was Phanan's glass prowler, he called Phanan over to take care of it, but it was not Phanan's. Realizing that someone had attempted to play a prank on them, Phanan and Loran drew up an elaborate revenge on Thri'ag, who they realized had to be the prankster. With Kell Tainer's aid, they set up a small speaker in Thri'ag's room set to play the noise of the glass prowler moving and sliced Thri'ag's room terminal to contain an entry on the [[Storini Crystal Deceiver]], a false creature which was supposedly quite similar to the glass prowler, relentless in its pursuit of its victims, and horribly deadly. They played the noise slightly on the first night, before returning to make the strike against the world, recorded by the spy satellites while masquerading as Rogue Squadron. The next night, they played the noise with increasing frequency until Thri'ag went to Phanan, in the next cabin, to request the use of some spray sealant Phanan had used on Storinal. The [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] slicer spent much of the night methodically sealing every crack in his room to keep the creature from getting out of the walls, where it seemed to be, then returned to bed. Phanan and Loran waited until he was asleep, then cut power to his cabin and played the sound from directly beneath the bed. The terrified Bothan leaped to his feet and ran to the door, which he had to pry open before an insect-like assembly swung into his face and he passed out. Thri'ag was taken to the medical bay, where Phanan examined him; when Thri'ag insisted he had been attacked by a Crystal Deceiver in front of several others, Phanan showed there was no such thing in the ship's [[Computer/Legends|computers]], making Thri'ag look ridiculous. Once the others left, Phanan and Loran admitted to the prank and forced Thri'ag to promise to end his own, which he agreed to do when the pair threatened to tell everyone he had fainted. At Talasea, Trigit was able to figure out that it would be a New Republic ambush during his fleet's hyperspace jump there; when the other two ships involved realized this, they turned to flee before the New Republic forces entered the [[Star system/Legends|system]]. ''Night Caller'' was forced to fire on them, with Phanan participating in the "[[Loran Spitball]]" when, on Loran's suggestion, all the X-wings in the bow hold fired their proton torpedoes at the ''[[Provocateur]]'' before launching; Phanan was the first to notice that the ''Night Caller''{{'s}} bridge took a direct hit while its [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] were down to launch the fighters. The Wraiths were able to destroy ''Provocateur'' and capture ''[[Constrictor]]'', and so the Wraiths were able to maintain the guise that the ''Night Caller'' had barely escaped the ambush. Zsinj had the ''Night Caller'' and Trigit set up an ambush of Rogue Squadron, supposedly following them, at [[Ession]]; the New Republic prepared a trap of its own. They ambushed Trigit's [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]], [[Implacable (Star Destroyer)/Legends|''Implacable'']], with another Loran Spitball before launching; the Wraiths harassed the now unshielded ''Implacable'', crippling it. When the ''Implacable''{{'s}} fighters returned, the Wraiths faced them with the aid of several other New Republic squadrons, but Phanan was pursued by a [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptor]] and his fighter was heavily damaged, forcing him to eject. Phanan was recovered and survived while his squadronmates destroyed the ''Implacable'' and killed Trigit, but Phanan's [[spleen]] was punctured by shrapnel while ejecting. Sandskimmer and Thri'ag were both lost in the final battle, however. ===A pirate's life=== {{Quote|When you put this idea in front of him, flames are going to come out of his mouth and burn you from head to foot.''"
"''That's true. But one thing makes it easier for me to take.''"
"''What's that?''"
"''You're going to be there burning with me.''"
"''You're such a good friend.|Ton Phanan and Garik Loran, on presenting their plan to Wedge Antilles|}} The squadron headed to [[Borleias/Legends|Borleias]] after the incident, where Phanan recovered in [[Medcenter/Legends|hospital]] from the loss of his spleen. There, he met [[Gara Petothel|Lara Notsil]], a young woman who had been a prisoner aboard ''Implacable'' before being rescued when it was destroyed. Once they recovered, they returned to headquarters at [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. One evening there, while on leave at a cantina, Ekwesh was struck by a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] attacker whom Tainer fought off while Phanan examined Ekwesh. Military police quickly arrived to hustle the Wraiths out of the fight, but Loran found their quick arrival and odd mannerisms suspicious, causing the Wraiths to strike out against their captors. Phanan used a [[laser scalpel]] to cut the throat of the man nearest him and took his [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]], forcing the others to surrender. The Wraiths decided Zsinj had likely been behind the retaliatory attack, and tightened up security. In the aftermath of the incident, Colonel Atton Repness had questioned them; Phanan remembered that as the name of Sarkin's crooked trainer and came up with a plan to ask Notsil to enter Repness's training program and lure him into a repeat of his previous offer, catching Repness in the act and bringing him to justice. Meanwhile, the Wraiths were assigned the task of brainstorming Zsinj's tactics, attempting to think as if they were Zsinj. Phanan was assigned to a planning group with Loran, Wes Janson, and new Wraith [[Dia Passik]]. While brainstorming, Phanan brought Loran in on the scheme to bring down Repness, and the two went to ask Notsil to cooperate. In order for her to agree, Loran had to promise to ask Antilles to give her a place in one of his squadrons after she graduated, as no one else would trust her. Antilles agreed that he would consider her for a position, which was good enough to begin the plot. The Wraiths continued brainstorming, and when their reports were turned in, it was decided that saBinring's theory that Zsinj was hiring pirates as a second [[Navy/Legends|navy]] of sorts was valid, and so the Wraiths were assigned to take on the identity of a pirate group and attempt to gain employment by Zsinj, gaining his confidence before betraying him. The Wraiths, taking on the alias of a pirate gang calling themselves the [[Hawk-bat Independent Space Force]], transferred to [[Halmad/Legends|Halmad]], where they based themselves out of an abandoned [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] facility. Phanan, still without an X-wing of his own, put in time in the TIE simulators, as the "Hawk-bats" would be using TIE craft for their deception. At Halmad, they had to steal more TIEs; in a play on the earlier attack on them, Phanan started a fight with Tainer in a Halmad cantina which spilled over to involve a squadron of [[TIE pilot]]s. The other Wraiths, dressed as military police, took the TIE pilots in and were so able to [[Halmad campaign|infiltrate]] the base. Phanan, now in [[Stormtrooper armor/Legends|stormtrooper armor]], took the place of one of the guards of the TIE [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] after the guards were stunned; they guarded the Wraiths as they took possession of six of the TIE interceptors within. During that period, a [[Skimmer/Legends|skimmer]] full of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] arrived for a scheduled shift change; Phanan and Loran were forced to fire on them, driving them off. Phanan piloted one of the stolen Interceptors out; he had never flown such a fighter before, and had difficulty keeping it under control, but successfully departed. With their new fighters, the Hawk-bats began raiding local shipping, killing no one but seizing a great deal of cargo. Phanan participated in these raids both as a fighter pilot and a member of the boarding parties. Meanwhile, Notsil was able to bring down Repness on her own, and performed sufficiently well for Antilles to bring her into the squadron. While the other Wraiths went off to perform a strike in conjunction with the [[Solo Fleet]] to maintain the illusion that the Wraiths were with that unit, Phanan remained behind with Tainer, Ekwesh, and the new slicer, [[Castin Donn]]. Exploiting a gap in Halmad's sensor coverage, they robbed a bank. When the others returned, Phanan had dinner with Notsil, an attractive woman, but could see that she was not interested in him; none of the women he had attempted to flirt with recently were. Deeply depressed by his lack of success with women, Phanan returned to his room and began drinking. Loran had realized that something was wrong and went to speak with Phanan, who confessed to his best friend that he had lost some part of himself when he was wounded at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]; he was convinced he had no future, and women no longer seemed interested in him. Loran attempted to comfort him, but Phanan maintained that he just could not see a bright future for himself. Loran confiscated his [[Alcohol/Legends|alcohol]] and left. ===An untimely death=== {{Quote|I was just imagining what a sad galaxy this would be without my superior intellect and general state of wonderfulness.|Ton Phanan, to Garik Loran|}} [[File:DeathofTonPhananPencils.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Garik Loran holds Phanan's body in his arms.]] The next day, the Wraiths set out as the Hawk-bats, capturing the fuel tanker [[Bastion (starship)|''Bastion'']] over Halmad before targeting a secret Imperial base south of the Halmad city of [[Fellon]]. At the base, they found an ambush waiting, three squadrons of fighters against what was not even a full squadron of Hawk-bats. In the [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfight]], Phanan was able to shoot down several enemies, but he took a hit to the [[Ion engine/Legends|engines]] and crashed in the forest. His landing was extremely rough and caused severe internal injuries, but he was able to drag himself from the wreck and crawl away before Zsinj's forces on the ground could reach him. He made it to a river and kept crawling until his trail led to stony ground where he would leave fewer traces, then doubled back to the river and began moving downstream. Knowing that attempting to protect Phanan's downed fighter would only cause all the Wraiths to fall, Antilles ordered them to break off and flee; Loran stayed behind, losing his pursuers in the forests and landing his ship covertly to search for Phanan on the ground. Loran, having stolen a [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] from some of Zsinj's [[Troop/Legends|troops]], figured out Phanan's strategy where Zsinj's men did not and was able to catch up with him. Putting Phanan on the speeder bike, which had lost power to its engines and simply floated, Loran began towing the wounded man while Phanan used a sensor unit to track their pursuit. When Loran, worried over Phanan's worsening condition, suggested that they simply surrender and get Phanan medical care immediately, Phanan refused, telling his friend that they were too recognizable and their capture would blow the Hawk-bats' cover. He considered stopping Zsinj more important than his own life, and made Loran promise that if he were to die, Loran would incinerate his body to keep it from being identified. Knowing that he was dying, Phanan spent his time making a will on his [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], and when he gave it to Loran he told his friend to stop punishing himself for the good he had done the Empire as a child. He also sent a message to Loran's account describing his injuries, telling him he knew he would die, and ordering him not to hold himself responsible for Phanan's death. He also left all his money to Loran, and thanked him for his close friendship. Some time later, Phanan saw [[Iron Fist (Executor-class)|''Iron Fist'']], Zsinj's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], in orbit and remarked on it to Loran, passing away almost immediately thereafter, while simply appreciating the [[Star/Legends|starry]] vista. Loran was heartbroken, but returned to his fighter the next morning and was able to escape after using its [[Laser/Legends|lasers]] to incinerate Phanan's corpse. He also received Phanan's last message. In accordance with the terms of Phanan's will, he had a surgical procedure to remove his distinctive scar, his physical punishment of himself. If he had not, the money would have gone to [[Tetran Cowall]], Loran's hated one-time rival as a child actor. Phanan's death hit Loran hard, but Phanan's words to him urging him not to punish himself were able to convince him to keep fighting in one battle when he would otherwise have let himself die. Phanan finally cured Loran of his desire to punish himself for his prior deeds.''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]'' ===Legacy=== {{Quote|They're commandos. Intelligence field agents. They're called Wraith Squadron. Their leader, a man named Loran, is dead. Their field leader, now in charge, is someone we thought for more than thirty years was dead. His name is Ton Phanan.|Stavin Thaal|''[[X-Wing: Mercy Kill]]''}} After the end of the Zsinj campaign, Loran was promoted to [[Captain/Legends|captain]] and given command of Wraith Squadron as it transferred to [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]]. The Wraiths continued to serve the New Republic and its successor, the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], until the unit was disbanded by [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] [[Natasi Daala]] around [[41 ABY]]. In [[44 ABY]], Loran was approached by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Borath Maddeus]] of [[Galactic Alliance Security]] to unofficially reform Wraith Squadron and investigate [[Chief of Army Operations]] General [[Stavin Thaal]]'s involvement in the [[Lecersen Conspiracy]], a recently exposed attempt by several senior [[Politics/Legends|politicians]] and [[Officer/Legends|officers]] to seize control of the Alliance and merge it into the [[Imperial Remnant]]. To protect the identities of the new Wraiths while they conducted their investigation, Loran sent Maddeus reports of a fake group made up of fictitious Wraiths and led by Phanan. When Thaal attempted to kill Loran and began searching for Phanan, Loran realized that Maddeus and Thaal were working together. Wraith Squadron lured Thaal to the planet [[Kuratooine]], where [[Turman Durra]], disguised as Phanan, exposed the general to Colonel [[Kadana Sorrel]] of [[Galactic Alliance Starfighter Command]], leading to the arrest of both Thaal and Maddeus. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|But something died when I was hit in that medical ward, and I think it was my future. I think people, maybe only women, can just look at me and say, 'There's no future in him.'''"
"''That's ridiculous.''"
"''There's no mechanical replacement for a future, Face. And every time I take a hit, and they have to cut away another part of me and replace it with machinery because I'm allergic to bacta, every time that happens I seem to be a little further away from the young doctor who had a future. He can't come back, Face. Not all of him is here anymore.|Ton Phanan and Garik Loran|}} Ton Phanan grew up a relatively normal man, if in possession of a roguish sense of humor. However, when he was injured over Endor, he fell into a state of depression as he felt his future had been cut out from under him. As he picked up more wounds and more cybernetics, he felt that he was losing something of himself; his earlier optimism was replaced with cynicism, bitterness, and depression over his situation. He was no longer able to find success with women, something he blamed on his self-perceived lack of a future. This was a particularly cutting loss to Phanan, who was something of a ladies' man.{{InsiderCite|59|Who's Who in Rogue Squadron}} Phanan covered his depression by adopting an exaggerated sense of humor, constantly making witty, sarcastic, or sly comments, puns, and jokes. Phanan hated the Empire, and sought to punish it directly for his injuries. While he could be flippant and informal with his superior officers, he always performed professionally in his actual capacity as a squadron member. He found refuge in friends such as Loran, but still felt haunted by the hollowness inside and was not able to find enough enjoyment in life to struggle for it on Halmad. Instead, he used his last acts to give Loran the peace of mind and psychological strength to overcome his own personal demons as Phanan never had. A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] with [[Color/Legends|blue]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], brown [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and fair skin, Phanan had a thin, well-trimmed mustache and beard during his time with Wraith Squadron. ==Equipment== {{Quote|I'm twenty percent mechanical, and gaining.|Ton Phanan|}} While assigned to Wraith Squadron, Phanan generally operated a T-65 X-wing starfighter, assisted by his R2-series astromech droid, Gadget. While engaged in piloting operations, Phanan wore a dark green [[Rebel flight suit|flight suit]]''[[Galaxy at War]]'' equipped with a personal [[Magnetic field/Legends|magcon field]] capable of protecting the pilot while extravehicular. When the Wraiths posed as the Hawk-bat pirate gang, Phanan had the opportunity to pilot Imperial TIE fighters and TIE interceptors and wore an all-gray version of the standard TIE pilot [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]]. Phanan typically carried a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and, in his role as Wraith Squadron's medic, was equipped with bandages, bacta treatments, [[disinfectant spray]], tranquilizers and a laser scalpel which could be used as a holdout weapon in emergencies. Phanan's allergy to bacta left him requiring prosthetic implants as a result of injuries sustained during his hazardous career. By the time he served with Wraith Squadron, Phanan was equipped with a prosthetic plate over the left of his face which featured a built-in [[Comm unit/Legends|comm unit]], a red mechanical left eye, a cybernetic left leg and a prosthetic right hand which resembled an organic Human hand. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|In essence, Ton and Face had something very important in common—they'd been so badly damaged by their experiences that they didn't have much of a future to look forward to. The difference between them was that Ton couldn't come back from what had damaged him, and Face could. Ton realized this, though Face did not. In dying, Ton was able to give Face some thoughts and words—and one symbol (the restoration of his face)—that could set Face on the path toward recovery.|Aaron Allston|{{WebCite|url=http://www.aaronallston.com/factpages/faq_starwars.html|text=Frequently Asked Questions: ''Star Wars'' Novels|work=[[Aaron Allston]]'s [https://web.archive.org/web/20150201124617/http://www.aaronallston.com official website]|archivedate=20150102092638|nolive=1}}}} The character of Ton Phanan was created by author [[Aaron Allston]] for ''[[X-Wing: Wraith Squadron]]'', the fifth book in the [[Star Wars: X-Wing (novel series)|X-Wing]] series, released in [[1998]]. Phanan and Loran were intended to act as wise-guy protaganists in the style of [[Wikipedia:Nick and Nora Charles|Nick and Nora Charles]] and several charcters from [[Wikipedia:Howard Hawks|Howard Hawks]] movies.{{CollectorCite|4|Rogue Leaders}} Allston has said that the Phanan-Loran duo are his favorite characters for comedic material. The characters developed a following among fans and were likened by some to Hawkeye Pierce and B.J. Hunnicutt from the television series [[Wikipedia:M*A*S*H (TV series)|M*A*S*H]]. Phanan was originally intended to die in the battle at M2398, but Allston decided Phanan had too much to contribute to the story before dying and substituted Jesmin Ackbar in his place. Allston ultimately decided that Phanan's death should give Loran the impetus to overcome his own issues and wrote his death in the subsequent novel, ''[[X-Wing: Iron Fist]]'', to fulfill this purpose. Phanan's death made the "''[[20 Most Memorable Moments of the Expanded Universe]]''" list in [[Star Wars Insider 83|''Star Wars Insider'' 83]]. The article mistakenly cited his death as occurring in ''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]'', however, rather than ''X-Wing: Iron Fist''. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Ton Phanan}} *''[[X-Wing: Wraith Squadron]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Wraith Squadron (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *[[X-Wing: Wraith Squadron (abridged audiobook)|''X-Wing: Wraith Squadron'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[X-Wing: Iron Fist]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Iron Fist (unabridged audiobook)}} *[[X-Wing: Iron Fist (abridged audiobook)|''X-Wing: Iron Fist'' abridged audiobook]] *''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Solo Command (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *''[[X-Wing: Mercy Kill]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Mercy Kill (audiobook)}} {{C|Impersonated}} ==Sources== *{{CollectorCite|4|Rogue Leaders}} *{{GamerCite|9|Wraith Squadron: The Dirty Dozen of Star Wars|Wraith Squadron: The Dirty Dozen of ''Star Wars''}} *{{InsiderCite|59|Who's Who in Rogue Squadron}} *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *{{InsiderCite|83|20 Most Memorable Moments of the Expanded Universe}} *{{InsiderCite|84|Comlink}} *''[[The Force Unleashed Campaign Guide]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Galaxy at War]]'' {{Po}} *{{InsiderCite|135|Attack Positions!}} *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' {{StartBox}} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Wraith Seven (callsign)|Wraith Seven]] |years=[[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]] |before=None |before-years= |after=[[Dia Passik]] |after-years=[[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]}} {{EndBox}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Ton Phanan }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Phanan, Ton}} [[Category:Alliance Fleet personnel]] [[Category:Cyborgs with prosthetics]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with lost limbs]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Medical professionals]] [[Category:Wraith Squadron personnel]]
